steps: Go to https://console.cloud.google.com and sign in with your Google account
Select an existing project from the top dropdown or click 'New Project' to create one
Go to https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/library/drive.googleapis.com and click 'Enable' to enable the Google Drive API
Click '+ Create Credentials' and select 'OAuth client ID'
If prompted, click 'Configure consent screen', choose 'External', fill in the app name and your email, add the Drive scopes you need, then save
Back on the credentials page, click '+ Create Credentials' > 'OAuth client ID'
Select 'Web application' as the application type
Enter a name (e.g. 'Drive API Client'), add your redirect URI under 'Authorized redirect URIs', then click 'Create'
Copy the Client ID and Client Secret from the dialog that appears

credential_fields.name: client_id
credential_fields.label: Client ID
credential_fields.description: The OAuth 2.0 Client ID from Google Cloud Console

credential_fields.name: client_secret
credential_fields.label: Client Secret
credential_fields.description: The OAuth 2.0 Client Secret from Google Cloud Console

notes: The Google Drive API is free with no billing required for basic usage
Usage limits apply: 1 billion requests/day with per-user quotas — see https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/api/drive.googleapis.com/quotas
Required OAuth scopes vary by operation — use https://www.googleapis.com/auth/drive for full access or more restricted scopes like drive.readonly or drive.file for least-privilege
The OAuth consent screen must be configured before creating OAuth credentials; sensitive scopes (drive, drive.readonly) require Google verification for production apps
Add test users on the consent screen page if your app is in 'Testing' mode (up to 100 test users allowed)

test_endpoint.description: Get basic information about the authenticated user and their Drive
